---
title: "Using WithLoaderData"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Using WithLoaderData}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

## Overview

`WithLoaderData()` is a generic wrapper component that connects React Router's
[`useLoaderData()`](https://reactrouter.com/6.30.0/hooks/use-loader-data) hook
to any `shiny.react`-based child component. It injects client-side loader data
into a specified prop of the child at render time — no Shiny server roundtrip
required.

This means you can use the full API of any React component (e.g.
`muiDataGrid::DataGrid()`, `muiMaterial::Typography()`) and let
`WithLoaderData()` supply the data from a JavaScript loader.

## `useLoaderData` vs `WithLoaderData`

Both components access React Router's `useLoaderData()` hook, but they differ
in origin and purpose:

`useLoaderData()` is a thin wrapper around React Router's own
[`useLoaderData()`](https://reactrouter.com/6.30.0/hooks/use-loader-data) hook.
It calls the hook and renders the result as text — it maps directly to the
upstream React Router API.

`WithLoaderData()` is a **custom helper created in this R package**. It does not
correspond to any React Router component. It was designed to bridge the gap
between React Router's loader system and `shiny.react`-based R packages by
injecting loader data as a structured JavaScript value into a child component's
prop via `React.cloneElement()`.

Because `WithLoaderData()` relies on `React.cloneElement()`, the child must be a
component built on the
[`shiny.react`](https://appsilon.github.io/shiny.react/) framework. Examples of
R packages that provide compatible components include:

- [`muiDataGrid`](https://github.com/anthropic/muiDataGrid) — MUI X Data Grid
- [`muiMaterial`](https://github.com/anthropic/muiMaterial) — MUI Material components
- [`muiCharts`](https://github.com/anthropic/muiCharts) — MUI X Charts
- [`muiTreeView`](https://github.com/anthropic/muiTreeView) — MUI X Tree View
- [`shiny.fluent`](https://appsilon.github.io/shiny.fluent/) — Microsoft Fluent
  UI components
- [`shiny.blueprint`](https://appsilon.github.io/shiny.blueprint/) — Palantir
  Blueprint UI components
- [`shinyNextUI`](https://github.com/anthropic/shinyNextUI) — NextUI components

Standard `htmltools` tags (`tags$div`, `tags$h3`, etc.) are **not**
`shiny.react` components, so they cannot be used as the `child` of
`WithLoaderData()`. For rendering loader data inside plain HTML elements, use
`useLoaderData()` instead.

| | `useLoaderData()` | `WithLoaderData()` |
|---|---|---|
| **Origin** | React Router hook (upstream API) | Custom wrapper (this package) |
| **Output** | Text or JSON string rendered directly in the DOM | Raw JS value (object, array, number) injected as a prop |
| **Use case** | Display loader values in HTML elements (`h3`, `p`, `span`, etc.) | Feed structured loader data into `shiny.react` component props (`rows`, `value`, `items`, etc.) |
| **Requires `prop`?** | No | Yes — you must specify which prop to inject into |
| **Requires `child`?** | No — it *is* the rendered output | Yes — needs a `shiny.react`-based component |

**Use `useLoaderData()` when you want to render a value as text:**

```r
tags$h3(useLoaderData(selector = "name"))      # renders "Alice"
tags$pre(useLoaderData())                      # renders full JSON string
```

**Use `WithLoaderData()` when a `shiny.react` component needs the data as a
structured JS object or array:**

```r
WithLoaderData(
  prop = "rows",
  field = "people",
  child = muiDataGrid::DataGrid()
)
```

The key distinction is data format: `useLoaderData()` always converts to a
string (via `String()` or `JSON.stringify()`), while `WithLoaderData()` passes
the raw JavaScript value. A data grid needs an actual array of objects for its
`rows` prop — a JSON string would not work. Conversely, for displaying a user's
name inside an `<h3>`, `useLoaderData(selector = "name")` is simpler and more
direct than wrapping a child component with `WithLoaderData()`.

## How it works

React Router [data routers](https://reactrouter.com/6.30.0/routers/picking-a-router)
(`createHashRouter`, `createBrowserRouter`, `createMemoryRouter`) support
`loader` functions — JavaScript functions that fetch data before a route renders.
Components on that route can access the loader's return value via the
`useLoaderData()` hook.

In plain React, you would write:

```js
function PeoplePage() {
  const data = useLoaderData();
  return <DataGrid rows={data.people} columns={columns} />;
}
```

Since R cannot call React hooks directly, `WithLoaderData()` bridges this gap.
It wraps a child component, calls `useLoaderData()` at render time, and injects
the result into whichever prop you specify via `React.cloneElement()`.

## Arguments

| Argument | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| `prop` | yes | The name of the child's prop to inject data into (e.g. `"rows"`, `"children"`, `"value"`). |
| `child` | yes | A `shiny.react`-based component that will receive the loader data as the specified prop. |
| `field` | no | A key to extract from the loader data object. If `NULL` (default), the entire loader data is passed. |

## Designing your loader

The React Router community recommends that each loader returns **exactly the
shape the consuming component needs**. This keeps things simple and avoids
the need for the `field` argument:

```r
library(reactRouter)
library(muiDataGrid)
library(htmltools)

# Loader returns the array directly — no field needed
loader <- JS("async () => {
  const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');
  return (await res.json()).map((p, i) => ({
    id: i, name: p.name, height: p.height
  }));
}")

columns <- JS("[
  { field: 'name',   headerName: 'Name',   flex: 1 },
  { field: 'height', headerName: 'Height', width: 100 }
]")

RouterProvider(
  Route(
    path = "/",
    loader = loader,
    element = div(
      style = "height: 400px;",
      WithLoaderData(
        prop = "rows",
        child = muiDataGrid::DataGrid(columns = columns)
      )
    )
  )
)
```

When a single loader serves multiple components on the same route, use `field`
to extract the relevant key:

```r
library(reactRouter)
library(muiDataGrid)
library(muiMaterial)
library(htmltools)

# Loader returns multiple datasets — use field to pick one
loader <- JS("async () => {
  const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');
  const people = await res.json();
  return {
    title: people.length + ' characters found',
    people: people.map((p, i) => ({
      id: i, name: p.name, height: p.height
    }))
  };
}")

columns <- JS("[
  { field: 'name',   headerName: 'Name',   flex: 1 },
  { field: 'height', headerName: 'Height', width: 100 }
]")

RouterProvider(
  Route(
    path = "/",
    loader = loader,
    element = div(
      WithLoaderData(
        prop = "children",
        field = "title",
        child = muiMaterial::Typography(variant = "h5")
      ),
      div(
        style = "height: 400px;",
        WithLoaderData(
          prop = "rows",
          field = "people",
          child = muiDataGrid::DataGrid(columns = columns)
        )
      )
    )
  )
)
```

However, the React Router best practice is to prefer **nested routes with their
own loaders** over a single loader returning multiple datasets. Each route
should own its data:

```r
library(reactRouter)
library(muiDataGrid)
library(muiMaterial)
library(htmltools)

stats_loader <- JS("async () => {
  const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');
  const people = await res.json();
  return { summary: people.length + ' characters in database' };
}")

people_loader <- JS("async () => {
  const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');
  return (await res.json()).map((p, i) => ({
    id: i, name: p.name, height: p.height
  }));
}")

columns <- JS("[
  { field: 'name',   headerName: 'Name',   flex: 1 },
  { field: 'height', headerName: 'Height', width: 100 }
]")

RouterProvider(
  Route(
    path = "/",
    loader = stats_loader,
    element = div(
      WithLoaderData(
        prop = "children",
        field = "summary",
        child = muiMaterial::Typography(variant = "h5")
      ),
      Outlet()
    ),
    Route(
      index = TRUE,
      loader = people_loader,
      element = div(
        style = "height: 400px;",
        WithLoaderData(
          prop = "rows",
          child = muiDataGrid::DataGrid(columns = columns)
        )
      )
    )
  )
)
```

## Works with any shiny.react component

`WithLoaderData()` is not tied to any specific package. It works with any
component built on `shiny.react`, because it simply injects a prop value
via `React.cloneElement()`. Here are some examples:

**muiDataGrid::DataGrid** — inject loader data as `rows`:

```r
library(reactRouter)
library(muiDataGrid)
library(htmltools)

loader <- JS("async () => {
  const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');
  return (await res.json()).map((p, i) => ({
    id: i, name: p.name, height: p.height, gender: p.gender
  }));
}")

RouterProvider(
  Route(
    path = "/",
    loader = loader,
    element = div(
      style = "height: 400px;",
      WithLoaderData(
        prop = "rows",
        child = muiDataGrid::DataGrid(
          columns = JS("[
            { field: 'name',   headerName: 'Name',   flex: 1 },
            { field: 'height', headerName: 'Height', width: 100 },
            { field: 'gender', headerName: 'Gender', width: 100 }
          ]"),
          pageSizeOptions = JS("[5, 10, 25]")
        )
      )
    )
  )
)
```

For interactive charts, you can inject loader data as `dataset` in **muiCharts** components:

```r
library(reactRouter)
library(muiCharts)
library(htmltools)

loader <- JS("async () => {
  const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');
  const people = await res.json();
  return people.slice(0, 10).map(p => ({
    name: p.name,
    height: Number(p.height) || 0,
    mass: Number(p.mass) || 0
  }));
}")

RouterProvider(
  Route(
    path = "/",
    loader = loader,
    element = WithLoaderData(
      prop = "dataset",
      child = muiCharts::BarChart(
        series = list(
          list(
            dataKey = "height", label = "Height (cm)"
          ),
          list(
            dataKey = "mass",   label = "Mass (kg)"
          )
        ),
        xAxis = list(
          list(scaleType = 'band', dataKey = 'name')
        ),
        height = 400
      )
    )
  )
)
```

**muiMaterial::Typography** — inject loader data as text content:

```r
library(reactRouter)
library(muiMaterial)
library(htmltools)

loader <- JS("async () => {
  return { title: 'Hello from the loader!' };
}")

RouterProvider(
  Route(
    path = "/",
    loader = loader,
    element = WithLoaderData(
      prop = "children",
      field = "title",
      child = muiMaterial::Typography(variant = "h1")
    )
  )
)
```

## Injecting into multiple props

If you need to inject loader data into more than one prop of the same component,
nest `WithLoaderData()` calls:

```r
library(reactRouter)
library(muiDataGrid)
library(htmltools)

loader <- JS("async () => {
  const res = await fetch('https://swapi.info/api/people');
  const people = await res.json();
  return {
    columnDefs: [
      { field: 'name', headerName: 'Name', flex: 1 },
      { field: 'height', headerName: 'Height', width: 100 }
    ],
    people: people.map((p, i) => ({ id: i, name: p.name, height: p.height }))
  };
}")

RouterProvider(
  Route(
    path = "/",
    loader = loader,
    element = div(
      style = "height: 400px;",
      WithLoaderData(
        prop = "columns",
        field = "columnDefs",
        child = WithLoaderData(
          prop = "rows",
          field = "people",
          child = muiDataGrid::DataGrid()
        )
      )
    )
  )
)
```

Each wrapper extracts its own `field` from the same `useLoaderData()` result and
injects it into the specified `prop`.

## Requirements

`WithLoaderData()` requires a **data router** (`createHashRouter`,
`createBrowserRouter`, or `createMemoryRouter`) because only data routers
support `loader` functions. It will not work with the component-based routers
(`HashRouter`, `MemoryRouter`).

The child component's JavaScript bundle must be loaded independently. For
example, `muiDataGrid::DataGrid()` automatically attaches its own HTML
dependency — `reactRouter` does not bundle it.
